Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Agents of Chaos 22 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. William Blake, Remote by the Sea 29 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Researchers find 3,500-year-old loom that reveals textile revolution 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Cherri – programming language that compiles to an Apple Shortuct 197 upvotes, 40 comments
  5. An NSFW filter for Marginalia search 75 upvotes, 14 comments
  6. Roulette Computers: Hidden Devices That Predict Spins 32 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. Fedware: Government apps that spy harder than the apps they ban 273 upvotes, 79 comments
  8. Do your own writing 242 upvotes, 75 comments
  9. How to turn anything into a router 522 upvotes, 192 comments
  10. I use Excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 260 upvotes, 102 comments
  11. Show HN: Coasts – Containerized Hosts for Agents 44 upvotes, 12 comments
  12. Seeing Like a Spreadsheet 44 upvotes, 12 comments
  13. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 181 upvotes, 73 comments
  14. The stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 99 upvotes, 41 comments
  16. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. Take better notes, by hand 147 upvotes, 68 comments
  18. Turning a MacBook into a touchscreen with $1 of hardware (2018) 105 upvotes, 48 comments
  19. FTC action against Match and OkCupid for deceiving users, sharing personal data 205 upvotes, 106 comments
  20. Recover Apple Keychain 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  21. A sea of sparks: Seeing radioactivity 38 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 253 upvotes, 147 comments
  23. Learn Claude Code by doing, not reading 37 upvotes, 15 comments
  24. Bird brains (2023) 272 upvotes, 175 comments
  25. OCR for construction documents does not work, we fixed it 97 upvotes, 63 comments
  26. Vulnerability research is cooked 92 upvotes, 62 comments
  27. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 217 upvotes, 178 comments
  28. In math, rigor is vital, but are digitized proofs taking it too far? 83 upvotes, 70 comments
  29. How Iran is making a mint from the current war 28 upvotes, 19 comments
  30. Car Seats as Contraception 24 upvotes, 31 comments

Last generated 6:00am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
